channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Low-noise optomechanical single phonon-photon conversion for quantum networks
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 05 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67956-2

Optomechanical crystals are promising building blocks for quantum networks but suffer from thermal mechanical noise. Here the authors demonstrate on-demand conversion of single phonons into high-purity telecom photons with low thermal noise and MHz-scale narrow bandwidth using a quasi-2D optomechanical system.
item.dc:title: Low-noise optomechanical single phonon-photon conversion for quantum networks
item.dc:creator: Liu Chen
Alexander Rolf Korsch
Cauê Moreno Kersul
Rodrigo Benevides
Yong Yu
Thiago P. Mayer Alegre
Simon Gröblacher

item.title: Harnessing strengthening-metastability synergy for extreme work hardening in additively manufactured titanium alloys
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 05 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67683-8

A new AM titanium alloy design tailors the metastability-strengthening synergy and achieves high yield strength with enhanced work hardening and ductility by leveraging a complete, sequential martensitic transformation pathway.
item.dc:title: Harnessing strengthening-metastability synergy for extreme work hardening in additively manufactured titanium alloys
item.dc:creator: Xuliang Chen
Youneng Xie
Tianlong Zhang
Zhihao Jiang
Xiangren Bai
Shun Xu
Jian Lu
